mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and AXL tyrosine kinases, blocking FLT3‑driven signaling and proliferation
additional_notes: Approved FLT3 inhibitor; MOLM13 (FLT3‑ITD) sensitive; recommended 11‑point 3‑fold series (0.01 nM–1 µM).

mechanism: Potent FLT3 kinase inhibitor, suppresses FLT3‑ITD signaling
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; very potent in FLT3‑ITD models; same dilution series as gilteritinib.

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 including some resistance alleles, blocking downstream proliferative signals
additional_notes: Active against FLT3‑ITD and some resistant mutants; suggested 11‑point 3‑fold series 0.01 nM–1 µM.

mechanism: Multikinase inhibitor including FLT3, inhibits FLT3‑driven proliferation
additional_notes: Approved for FLT3‑mutant AML in combination; expected cellular IC50 ~10–200 nM; test range 0.1 nM–10 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases (including some FLT3 activity) to reduce AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Used off‑label/experimentally in FLT3‑ITD models; expected IC50 ~10–500 nM; test range 0.1 nM–10 µM.

mechanism: Broad tyrosine kinase inhibitor with reported activity against FLT3 and related kinases
additional_notes: Potent multi‑kinase inhibitor; reported FLT3 activity in some studies; suggested 0.01 nM–1 µM series.

mechanism: Selective BCL‑2 inhibitor that induces apoptosis by relieving BCL‑2–mediated caspase inhibition
additional_notes: Approved in AML combinations; cellular IC50 line‑dependent (~1–100 nM); test 0.01 nM–1 µM.

mechanism: Reversible inhibition of the 26S proteasome, causing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Single‑agent cytotoxicity in AML cell lines reported; expect ~1–100 nM IC50.

mechanism: Irreversible proteasome (20S) inhibitor causing accumulation of misfolded proteins and cell death
additional_notes: More potent proteasome inhibitor vs bortezomib; similar rationale for AML testing.

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase leading to oxidative stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Repurposed anticancer candidate with reported AML activity in low µM; suggested series spans ~0.001 µM (1 nM) to 50 µM; check solubility; many labs use top 30–50 µM.

mechanism: Disrupts mitochondrial function and modulates Wnt/STAT3 and other pathways leading to cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Reported AML and stem‑like cell activity (low µM); practical focus often 1 nM–10 µM.

mechanism: Binds tubulin, destabilizes microtubules, leading to mitotic arrest and cell death
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate with AML cytotoxicity reported in sub‑µM to low‑µM range; test 0.01 nM–50 µM or narrower around expected IC50.

mechanism: Multiple proposed effects (lysosomal/mitochondrial perturbation, ROS) causing cell death
additional_notes: Reported antileukemic effects in cell lines; expected IC50 ~1–20 µM; practical series 1 nM–50 µM.

category: Aldehyde dehydrogenase inhibitor / repurposing anticancer agent
mechanism: Forms Cu‑complexes that induce proteotoxicity/ubiquitin–proteasome disruption and kill cancer cells
additional_notes: Activity often copper‑dependent — test ±Cu (e.g., 1:1 molar ratio or 1–5 µM CuSO4); expected active range with Cu ~0.1–5 µM.

mechanism: Disrupts mitochondrial function and induces ER stress/NF‑κB modulation leading to cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Reported AML cell activity; expected IC50 ~0.1–10 µM; test 0.01 nM–50 µM (practical 0.1 nM–10 µM).

mechanism: Multiple anticancer effects (hedgehog, VEGF/angiogenesis inhibition, other pathways) that can impair AML cell survival
additional_notes: Reported anticancer activity and some AML sensitivity; test 0.01 nM–50 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits HMG‑CoA reductase, blocking cholesterol synthesis and protein prenylation leading to apoptosis
additional_notes: Statins induce apoptosis in AML by blocking prenylation; use active hydroxy acid form or convert lactone; expected IC50 ~1–50 µM; recommended 0.01–100 µM series.

mechanism: Exerts ER‑dependent/independent pro‑apoptotic effects including PKC/mitochondrial pathways in AML
additional_notes: Shows pro‑apoptotic effects in some AML lines; expected IC50 ~1–20 µM; practical testing 0.1 nM–50 µM.

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and inhibits autophagy, potentially sensitizing AML cells to stress and other agents
additional_notes: Often used in combination strategies; single‑agent effects frequently at high µM; suggested 0.1–200 µM (adjust upper limit based on assay).

mechanism: Alters mitochondrial metabolism/activates AMPK, impairing cancer cell metabolic fitness
additional_notes: Single‑agent cytotoxicity in vitro often requires mM concentrations; note potential media pH effects at high concentrations; test 0.01–50 mM.
